title: Catalog-wide residual dark halo audit
oneline: 48s offline auditor ranks all 94 night fills, finds 3 new real halos
summary: Generalized idea #1's reference-punch rim metric catalog-wide: every shipped night fill is scored on band-1..2 pixels that are >40 luma darker than a dilate-4 reference punch AND mid-dark (luma 55-145, idea #1's penumbra window — without it deliberate near-black art dominates the ranking). The audit found previously-unknown real halos (objects/teddy-wide smile shadow, farm/duck-tall re-inked mouth, a smudged star on dinosaur/pterodactyl-tall) and works as a regression gate: re-running with idea #1's rim-erase applied moved exactly the 4 lineW-gated pages' scores (train-wide 0.269 to 0.099) and left the other 90 byte-identical. Surprise: train-wide, the page that motivated the idea, ranks only #33 — its halo is locally severe but small, and duck-tall (lineW 173) proves the page-median lineW gate misses localized re-inking that this band metric catches.
tried: Reference-punch rimΔ metric (dilate-4 mask + neighbor bleed) generalized to all 94 shipped night fills
Naive unwindowed rimΔ>40 share as the page score
Mid-dark luma window [55,145) from idea #1 layered onto the rimΔ test
64px-tile hotspot detection + composite crops (compositeNight, 4x nearest) of the worst 12
Regression-gate demo: full second run with idea #1's rim-erase applied to lineW<150 pages, scores diffed
Ungated rim-erase on the two newly found true positives (teddy-wide, duck-tall)
worked: Full-catalog run in ~48s, offline, deterministic (unchanged pages reproduce bit-identical scores)
Mid-dark window fixed the ranking (farm/cow-wide rawScore 3.60 -> haloScore 0.63; black patches excluded)
Found 3 real shipped halos nobody had checked: teddy-wide smile shadow, duck-tall re-inked mouth outline, pterodactyl-tall star smudge
Regression gate: exactly the 4 gated pages moved (train-wide 0.269->0.099, duck-wide 0.161->0.003, caterpillar-wide 0.124->0, trex-wide 0.113->0.083), 90 others byte-identical
Healthy pages score ~0 (33 pages <= 0.05) — the metric doesn't fire on clean bright-line art
failed: train-wide does NOT rank in the worst 10 (it is #33 by haloScore, #22 by hotspot density) — its halo is real but small-area, so the 'verify it ranks where expected' expectation was wrong, not the metric (numbers match idea #1's measurements)

Local statistics cannot separate deliberate mid-dark art hugging lines (cat-wide bale straps #1, tire rings, spot rings) from halo — top-10 precision is ~30-40%, human crop review is the disambiguator
Ungated rim-erase barely helps duck-tall (outline re-inked 6-10px thick, beyond the 2px erase) — that page needs a raw regen, not a punch tweak
limitations: haloScore is page-area share, diluting localized failures; consumers should also read the per-page hotspot list
Scores at asset scale (1536x1024), not after downscale to device CSS pixels
The [55,145) window and rimΔ>40 threshold are inherited from idea #1's single-page tuning, not re-optimized catalog-wide
Needs fill-src raws (repo-only, not shipped), so it audits the pipeline workspace, not a deployed app
